verdict.answer_first: A: sentence 1 gives the bottom line, "almost certainly a timezone boundary bug"; B also does, but adds an unverified superlative, "single most common date bug".
verdict.decisiveness: A is more focused: "store and render it as a plain YYYY-MM-DD string or a date-only type"; B spreads into multiple tactics and libraries, "slicing... UTC accessors... toLocaleDateString... Temporal... date-fns/Luxon".
verdict.fabrication: B invents context: "That's why your US users complain and your European users don't." A: none.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on because it gives the mechanism, one concrete repro example, confirmation, and the core rule: "If the value is a true calendar date... never timezone-converted."
